summary: Defined the reusable repo automation contract before copying or moving any files. docs/workflows/repo-automation.md now names the external target, one-way sync direction from Owlory to repo-automation, reusable harness inventory, Owlory-specific exclusions, manifest contract, sync contract, automatic update behavior, and consumer repository responsibilities.
missing_proof_levels: Sync tooling, external repo bootstrap, automatic update hook, and consumer adoption proof remain queued in later slices.

contract_status_changes.contract: Reusable automation target and source of truth
contract_status_changes.before: The external repo-automation folder existed, but there was no documented sync direction or ownership contract.
contract_status_changes.after: Owlory is documented as the initial source of truth and /Users/raelldottin/Documents/Personal/repo-automation as the one-way reusable distribution target until an explicit ownership flip.

contract_status_changes.before: The queue named a future extraction, but the reusable and excluded asset classes were not documented.
contract_status_changes.after: Reusable harness assets, parameterization candidates, and excluded Owlory-specific queue, handoff, proof, localization, release, app, and product state are documented.

contract_status_changes.before: The next slice could have guessed which files to sync or touched the external folder directly.
contract_status_changes.after: The manifest, --check/--sync behavior, stale-file limits, and no-hook/no-bootstrap boundary for repo-automation-sync-tooling are documented.

residual_risks: The reusable automation package is not implemented yet; this slice only defines the contract for the following implementation slices.
recommended_next_reason: The reusable/excluded boundary and target contract are now documented, so the next adjacent step is the manifest-driven sync tool and tests.
